title: transcript::sanitize_path produces filesystem collisions for distinct paths
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/transcript.rs:88-101 (sanitize_path, project_dir_for)
## Violation
```rust
pub fn sanitize_path(name: &str) -> String {
    name.chars()
        .map(|c| if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() { c } else { '-' })
        .collect()
}
```

Replacing every non-alphanumeric byte with `-` is information-destroying. `/home/doll/Open-Claudia` and `/home/doll/Open Claudia` and `/home/doll/Open/Claudia` all collapse to `-home-doll-Open-Claudia`. The resulting directory `project_dir_for(cwd)` is shared by every collision target. Two distinct projects therefore write transcripts into the same directory, and `list_transcripts` will surface them as a single project's sessions — including their `gitBranch` and first-prompt text. For multi-tenant home directories or path-similar repos (`~/work/foo` vs `~/work/foo-bar` where the dash-target collides), this leaks one project's session metadata into another.

There is also no length cap: `sanitize_path("/very/long/path/...")` produces an arbitrarily long directory name. On Linux ext4 the path-component limit is 255 bytes; longer values fail at `create_dir_all` with no recovery, breaking transcript persistence silently.
## Mandated refactor
Append a short stable hash of the original `cwd` to the sanitized name (e.g. `-home-doll-OpenClaudia-d41d8cd98f00b204` using a 16-char hex of `blake3(cwd)`). This preserves human-readability AND uniqueness. Cap the prefix component at 200 bytes so the hash-suffixed total stays under filesystem limits. Add a regression test for the collision case (`/foo/bar` vs `/foo-bar`) and the long-path case.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
